TextENGNew: Rika-chan and I headed to the place we'd arranged to meet Shion over the phone.
changeReason: Old says 'arrived at the location'; new says 'headed to the place'. JPN 向かった = 'headed toward', indicating travel in progress, not arrival.

TextENGNew: ``Let's see... turn at the corner past this boutique...``
``...Keiichi, that's the one.``
I looked toward where Rika-chan was pointing. Sure enough, there was the coffee shop Shion had mentioned.
The door was made of pitch-black glass, making it impossible to see what lay inside.
I'd rarely set foot in a coffee shop, even with my parents... but beyond that, this place gave off an unwelcoming aura that seemed to repel people entirely.
My first impression, at least, was that this didn't look like the kind of place kids could casually walk into.
``Even so...``
That Shion -- she sure picked a strange spot for us.
In a decent-sized town like Okinomiya, there had to be plenty of cute coffee shops more to Shion's taste.
...Thinking that, I looked around wondering if maybe we'd come to the wrong place, but there were no other coffee shops in sight.
...I'd told her this was a secret meeting we needed to keep from Mion and the others, so maybe she'd deliberately chosen somewhere discreet? Well, whatever.
Pasted on the blacked-out windows was a sign advertising discounted tea for female customers on certain days of the week.
...Even so, no matter how they advertised, I doubted many women would willingly step into a grimy, suspicious-looking place like this.
As I stood there mulling that over, I suddenly realized Rika-chan, who should have been right beside me, was gone.
``...Wait, Rika-chan?``
When I looked toward the shop, I could see Rika-chan opening the door and walking inside without a moment's hesitation. I hurried after her and pulled open the dark glass door.
*clink*, *clink*...
A bell mounted on the door rang with a dull tone, announcing our arrival.
The moment I stepped inside, a foul stench hit my nose.
The interior was worse than I'd imagined. The air reeked of cigarettes and hung in a hazy, stagnant white pall. This was less a coffee shop and more a pachinko parlor or mahjong den.
Even so, in stark contrast to my hesitation, Rika-chan toddled right up to the owner standing behind the counter and looked up at him with her trademark ``nipah~`` smile.
The unfriendly owner showed neither surprise nor confusion, and wordlessly guided us to a table in the corner.
...I took my seat and surveyed the interior again. No matter how you looked at it, this was not the kind of place where people relaxed over cigarettes and coffee.
I couldn't see any other customers, but empty glasses and cups sat abandoned on the counter.
Without so much as taking our order, the owner set two glasses of water -- not even ice water -- on our table, retreated behind the counter, and switched on the television.
``Shion sure picked a terrible spot to drag us to, honestly...``

I grumbled in a voice just low enough that the owner wouldn't hear. The table was marked with ring stains from saucers and seemed vaguely dusty. It didn't look like it had been properly wiped down, and I hesitated to even rest my elbows on it.
``...Keiichi.``

Rika-chan took a sip of her water, cast a quick glance toward the owner, then leaned in close. The cheerful smile she'd been wearing vanished without a trace, replaced by a dead-serious expression as she spoke.
``...Can I really leave this entirely to you, Keiichi?``
``Yeah, of course.@k@vS14/01/210100643. I've been rehearsing, just in case...@k@vS14/01/210100644. But Rika-chan, if things start going south, I'm counting on you for backup.``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, altering the specificity of the passage.

TextENGNew: ``...Mii. I think you could at least tell me your plan beforehand.@k@vS14/05/210500492. I don't see any point in keeping secrets from me...``
Rika-chan furrowed her brow as she said that, looking displeased.
I still hadn't revealed my secret plan to Rika-chan -- my idea to enlist Shion's help in derailing Rina's scheme before it could be set in motion.
Part of the reason was that I wasn't fully confident in it yet, but there was something more than that.
``...Honestly, I still don't believe in this 'fate' you keep talking about, Rika-chan.``
``So won't you just watch?@k@vS14/01/210100647. I'll prove it to you -- that fate is far easier to change than you think.``
``...Keiichi, do you still doubt what I've been telling you?``

``No, that's not it.@k@vS14/01/210100649. It's precisely because I believe you that I want to prove it can be changed.@k@vS14/01/210100650. That we don't have to just grit our teeth and accept it -- that we have plenty of other options!``
``......Very well.``
Rika-chan nodded, seeming at least tentatively persuaded.
``Besides, if Shion finds out this involves Satoko, she'll definitely take it seriously.@k@vS14/01/210100652. It might be better to make sure she knows that upfront.``
changeReason: The level of certainty expressed differs significantly between old and new translations.

TextENGNew: Shion was always teasing and fooling around, but she truly cared about Satoko.
That was precisely why Satoko engaged with Shion so earnestly, even while putting on a show of annoyance on the surface.
Given the nature of their relationship, I was starting to feel it would be best to just be straightforward with Shion.
But Rika-chan didn't seem to agree. She lowered her gaze, looking troubled.
``You probably don't know this, Keiichi, but...@k@vS14/05/210500497. Once Shii sets her mind on a single goal, she won't hesitate to use any means necessary to achieve it.``
``If someone like Shii were to learn that Satoko had been suffering -- or that someone was going to make her suffer -- what do you think would happen?``
``She would certainly choose the most direct approach over anything indirect.@k@vS14/05/210500500. The single choice... that seems the most certain and, at first glance, the most efficient.``
``The most direct... You mean--``

For an instant, I realized where my own thoughts had led, and a chill ran down my spine. It was like some abominable nightmare... or perhaps something that had actually happened in another world I might have walked through--
And as if to confirm that thought, Rika-chan looked up and gave the faintest of nods.
``If only Rina weren't here.@k@vS14/05/210500502. If only Teppei weren't here.``
changeReason: Old: 'Just without Rina. Just without Teppei.'; new: 'If only Rina weren't here. If only Teppei weren't here.' Old sounds like listing; new expresses wishful thinking matching JPN さえいなければ (if only X weren't here).

TextENGNew: ``...The conclusion that line of thinking leads to is exactly what you just imagined, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500504. And the means chosen to avert one tragedy only give rise to yet another...``
``...Rika-chan?``
``......Such a world truly did exist.``
Rika-chan's expression turned solemn as she said that. ...It seemed, somehow, like she was remembering something -- and just watching her filled my chest with an aching, heartrending pain.
``Then... should we keep quiet about Satoko?``
``...We could tell her, but it might ignite a misguided kindness in Shii.@k@vS14/05/210500507. ...That is the one thing that absolutely must not happen, no matter what.``
``Rika-chan...``
``Keeping Shii from going out of control is also a crucial part of today's conversation.``
As usual, I didn't fully grasp what Rika-chan was driving at... but seeing her like that steeled my resolve all over again.

Right. Whether or not I believed what Rika-chan had told me, if this woman Rina's scheme actually came to pass, there was a high chance she'd vanish from Okinomiya regardless of whether it succeeded or failed.
And if that happened, Teppei would return to the Houjou household and try to drag Satoko back to support his lifestyle.
From what Rika-chan had told me, Satoko's life while living with her uncle and his wife last year had been so horrific that 'awful' didn't begin to cover it.
...To be honest, even now I was only half-convinced by Rika-chan's story. But what if there was even a one percent chance that Satoko would be plunged into that misery...?
changeReason: Numerical values differ between old ({'1'}) and new (set()) translations.

TextENGNew: And what if my actions right now could prevent that? ...There was nothing to think about. If Teppei Houjou were simply erased from this world, the whole problem would be solved...!
``...Whoa, no no no.``
I shook my head, banishing the thought that had just surfaced. Sure, it seemed like a good solution, and on the surface it appeared to be the quickest, most efficient choice -- at first glance.
And yet, I reconsidered. Would that truly be for Satoko's happiness?
Claiming it was for her sake while really just offloading my own guilt -- I could hear 'myself' screaming that from somewhere deep in my heart.
``Right... For now, just leave it to me.``
Now... this was where the real battle began.
Just as I started to gather my thoughts, the bell on the door rang out with a clatter.
Shion...?
I looked toward the entrance... but it wasn't her. It was a hard-faced man who fit right into a place like this.

He was impeccably dressed in a black suit from head to toe, his eyes hidden behind black sunglasses. His clothing combined with his stern features made it immediately obvious he was no ordinary civilian. He radiated an unmistakable aura of intimidation.
changeReason: Old: 'They were clearly an unusual form of intimidation'; new: 'He radiated an unmistakable aura of intimidation.' Old says 'they' (plural); new correctly says 'he' (singular) matching JPN singular subject.

TextENGNew: When I say he looked rough, I don't mean on the level of a street punk or some tough guy. ...He clearly exuded the aura of a professional.
The moment the owner noticed the man, he frantically switched off the TV, put on an obsequious expression he hadn't shown us, and greeted the newcomer while rubbing his hands together.
``W-welcome... please, come in.``
Was this man a regular? Or was there some kind of hierarchical relationship? The man in the suit surveyed the interior without a care, then glanced up at the menu board on the wall.
``...Barkeep.``
changeReason: Old: 'Host'; new: 'Barkeep'. JPN マスター can mean either, but context of a dingy bar-like cafe makes 'barkeep' more fitting.

TextENGNew: ``Y-yes? What can I do for you...?``
A bead of sweat trickled down the owner's forehead.
A stiff, forced smile was plastered across his sweating face.
He ran a place like this. He had to be used to dealing with people who lived on the wrong side of the law. For even this owner to be rattled this badly, the man had to be someone of real importance.
``...That thing, you know, the...``
``...Sw... sw... sw...@k@vS14/15/2115000004. ...That sweet-something-or-other -- are you serving those today?``
``Ah... ahahahaha.@k@vS14/00/mascafe10005. O-of course we are.@k@vS14/00/mascafe10006. I'll have it ready right away...``
The owner, who had been tensing up as if bracing for some complaint, seemed to exhale with relief as he hurried behind the counter.
...Wait. Did that guy just say 'sweet'...?
``Ahahaha~! Yes!@k@vS14/06/210600231. It's been forever since I've had one -- a Sweet Strawberry Compote Parfait!!``
changeReason: Old translates a strawberry parfait order; same meaning but 'Sweet Strawberry Compote Parfait' is capitalized as a proper menu item name in new, and phrasing differs.

TextENGNew: A bright, cheerful voice suddenly rang out, completely at odds with the atmosphere of the shop.
...Hold on, that voice...?
``Shion?``
``Oh, Kei-chan, Rika-chama!@k@vS14/06/210600233. Sorry to keep you waiting~!``
Accompanying that vibrant voice, Shion emerged from behind the man in the black suit. The moment she appeared, the dingy atmosphere of this backstreet cafe seemed to brighten in an instant.
changeReason: Old: 'Shion emerged from behind the man in the black suit, shouting in a lavish voice'; new: 'Accompanying that vibrant voice, Shion emerged from behind the man in the black suit.' Old says 'shouting' which implies louder volume than JPN 華やかな声 (vivid/gorgeous voice).

TextENGNew: Shion and the man in the black suit walked straight toward our table. Rika-chan gave a small, polite bow.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, altering the specificity of the passage.

TextENGNew: ``Good afternoon.@k@vS14/05/210500511. We just got here ourselves.``
Shion took a seat at the table, and the man in the suit settled heavily into the chair beside her.
...The man had an incredible presence. The sheer volume of intimidating aura he was giving off was anything but ordinary...
``Hey, Shion...``
``That person over there... um...@k@vS14/01/210100662. How should I put this...``
I carefully searched for the right words. How exactly was I supposed to describe this scary-looking man who practically screamed 'yakuza'?
But before I could find them.
Shion laughed cheerfully and waved her hand dismissively.
``You mean Kasai, right?@k@vS14/06/210600237. Well, just think of him as my bodyguard.``
``Bodyguard...?``
Walking around with a personal bodyguard -- Shion really was something else. It reminded me all over again just how much power the Sonozaki family wielded, even here in Okinomiya.

The man called Kasai straightened his posture and turned to face me, dipping his chin slightly in a bow without the faintest hint of a smile. ...Somehow, there wasn't a single opening in him, not even in a simple greeting.
``My name is Kasai.@k@vS14/15/2115000006. I understand Shion-san is always in your care...``
``If anything, I'm the one looking after them.@k@vS14/06/210600239. Right, Kei-chan?``
``Ah, hahahaha...@k@vS14/01/210100665. Nice to meet you. I'm Keiichi.``
I managed an awkward laugh. Even as I introduced myself, I found it hard to look Kasai-san directly in the face. ...Up close, his presence was twice as overwhelming.
``Something the matter, Kei-chan?``
``N-no... it's nothing.``
``Ahahaha, don't be so tense.@k@vS14/06/210600242. He looks scary, but he's a good guy, I promise.``
``I-is that so.@k@vS14/01/210100667. Ahahahaha...``

``I was planning to come alone today, actually.@k@vS14/06/210600244. But then I spotted this suspicious-looking guy at the library and, lo and behold, it was him. What a coincidence.@k@vS14/06/210600245. So I dragged him along as my walking wallet.``
Walking wallet... What kind of reaction was she expecting besides a laugh?
``Don't you think he's quite the catch?@k@vS14/06/210600247. You too, Rika-chama.``
changeReason: Old: 'Don't you think he's a good guy?'; new: 'Don't you think he's quite the catch?' JPN いい男 = 'good-looking man/catch', about attractiveness not just being nice.

TextENGNew: ``Mii~! Men in suits are wonderful.@k@vS14/05/210500513. And also...``
``What we came to discuss involves the Sonozakis, so this works out perfectly.``
``...It involves the Sonozakis?``
``That's right.``
Rika-chan smiled, glancing back and forth between Shion and Kasai-san. Shion blinked in puzzlement and tilted her head, then shrugged as if to say 'oh well.'
``Hmm... you mentioned a little over the phone, but I'm curious what this is all about.@k@vS14/06/210600251. Well, let's enjoy some sweets first!``
Shion flagged down the taciturn owner and ordered sweets for everyone.
Even for Kasai-san and me...
Before long, four vibrant parfaits -- rivaling anything from Angel Mort -- were ceremoniously brought to our table, and Rika-chan and Shion dug in happily.
...If the atmosphere weren't so awful, they'd look even more appetizing. Thinking that, I scooped out some ice cream with the long spoon.
It was actually good. This was an unexpected discovery.
Come to think of it, Shion worked at Angel Mort, so she had to be pretty particular about sweets. If she was ordering from here, the quality was practically guaranteed.
...It was such a shame about the atmosphere of this place, though. Such a waste.
I glanced over at Kasai-san... and found him elegantly sipping black coffee, his parfait completely untouched. ...When had he even ordered coffee?
``How is it? Surprisingly good, right?@k@vS14/06/210600253. The owner here doesn't look it, but he's actually quite skilled.``
``It's delicious. Nipah~!``
``Come on, Kasai, at least try a bite.@k@vS14/06/210600256. Heheheh!``
``Haha. I appreciate the offer, but I'm here as your escort today, Shion-san.@k@vS14/15/2115000009. I'll give it a try next time I come with a lady.``
``Ohhh? Is that so~?@k@vS14/06/210600258. What kind of woman, other than an 'employee,' would you bring here?@k@vS14/06/210600259. I'd love to see that.``
``Ahahahaha!``
Even Shion's teasing only produced the faintest smile at the corners of his mouth. Part of it was probably their long acquaintance, but more than that, it spoke to his depth and composure as a man.
``...So.@k@vS14/06/210600262. What did you want to talk about?``
The instant Shion -- who had polished off her parfait in no time -- spoke, the mood at the table solidified like a drawn bowstring. That's right. We'd come here to discuss something important.
``Right, about that...``
Now, how should I explain this...?
``Huh? Where's this coming from all of a sudden?``
Shion's eyes widened slightly as she tilted her head. Seeing that, Rika-chan set down her spoon and picked up where I left off.
``This concerns a woman named Rina Mamiya, who works at one of the Sonozaki-affiliated establishments.``
``A Sonozaki-affiliated place... Rina?@k@vS14/06/210600265. ...Doesn't ring a bell.@k@vS14/06/210600266. Kasai?``
Kasai-san lowered his gaze as if thinking it over. After a brief moment of deliberation over whether to speak, he answered.
``...What's this all of a sudden?``
Shion openly furrowed her brow, as though she'd heard an unpleasant name, and tilted her head suspiciously. Rika-chan set down her spoon and picked up where I left off.
``There is an acquaintance of Teppei's -- a woman named Rina Mamiya.@k@vS14/05/210500519. ...You haven't heard of her?``
``Rina... Mamiya?@k@vS14/06/210600269. ...Doesn't ring a bell.@k@vS14/06/210600270. What about her?``
``She apparently works at one of the Sonozaki-affiliated establishments...@k@vS14/01/210100671. You really don't know her?``
At Shion's prompting, Kasai-san lowered his gaze as if thinking it over. After a brief moment of deliberation over whether to speak, he answered.
``...I believe I know who you're referring to.@k@vS14/15/2115000011. ...Has something happened with Rina Mamiya?``
Kasai-san's tone was businesslike. His expression was unreadable behind those sunglasses, but from the timbre of his voice, I could sense his puzzlement at hearing Rina's name so unexpectedly.
Would the two of them take what we were about to say seriously? More importantly, would they even believe a story that sounded like some outlandish premonition?
Facing an adult like Kasai-san, I suddenly felt a wave of anxiety. ...But I couldn't afford to hesitate. I had to make them believe me.
I drew a small breath and spoke in one rush.
``The affiliated establishments pay monthly tribute to the Sonozakis, right?``
changeReason: Old: 'That bar makes treasury payments to the Sonozakis every month'; new: 'The affiliated establishments pay monthly tribute to the Sonozakis.' JPN 上納金 = tribute (yakuza context), not 'treasury payments'. Also old specifies 'bar' while new says 'establishments' matching JPN 系列の店.

TextENGNew: ``Rina is planning to skim those tribute funds.``
changeReason: Old: 'Rina is planning to steal those funds'; new: 'Rina is planning to skim those tribute funds.' 'Steal' vs 'skim' -- JPN 手をつけようと = 'to lay hands on/tamper with', which 'skim' captures better (embezzlement vs outright theft).

TextENGNew: Shion and Kasai-san exchanged blank looks. A perfectly natural reaction. They could never have expected something like this to come from my mouth.
``W-wait just a moment.@k@vS14/06/210600275. Why are we hearing this from Kei-chan and Rika-chama...?``
``W-well, that's...``
``And stealing tribute funds is no small matter, you know?@k@vS14/06/210600277. To begin with, who even is this Rina woman?``
Shion's last question was directed at Kasai-san. He adjusted his sunglasses with one hand as he answered.

``...Rina Mamiya.@k@vS14/15/2115000014. That's the name she goes by at the establishment, though whether it's her real name is unclear.@k@vS14/15/2115000015. She's a hostess working at one of the Flower Road clubs.``
changeReason: Old: 'She's an employee working at one of the Flower Road establishments'; new: 'She's a hostess working at one of the Flower Road clubs.' JPN 従業員 in yakuza context specifically means hostess/bar worker, not generic 'employee'.

TextENGNew: ``A 'hostess'?... Aah.``
changeReason: Old: 'Employee?'; new: 'A hostess?' Same reasoning - 従業員 in context means hostess.

TextENGNew: ``But that makes even less sense.@k@vS14/06/210600280. Why would Kei-chan and the others know anything about one of our hostesses?``
changeReason: Old: 'Why would Kei-chan and the others know about one of our employees?'; new: 'Why would Kei-chan and the others know anything about one of our hostesses?' Same terminology shift.

TextENGNew: ``Ah, well, it's because...``
Shion's sharp, quick-witted eyes darted back and forth between Rika-chan and me.
Meanwhile, Kasai-san sat perfectly still, his gaze fixed on my face. He seemed to be weighing both the truth and the intent behind my words.
``...I overheard it.``
As I struggled to answer, Rika-chan spoke up in a calm voice while scooping a bite of her half-melted parfait.
``Huh? You did, Rika-chama?@k@vS14/06/210600282. ...Where?``
``At the library.@k@vS14/05/210500522. She was chatting away loudly with some suspicious-looking people, having a grand old time.``
``...A-at the library?@k@vS14/06/210600284. One of our hostesses?``
changeReason: Old: 'One of our employees?'; new: 'One of our hostesses?' Same terminology.

TextENGNew: ``Ahahaha, that doesn't suit her at all~!``
changeReason: Old: 'that doesn't suit them at all'; new: 'that doesn't suit her at all.' JPN singular, old uses plural.

TextENGNew: ``Mii, she was completely out of place.``
changeReason: Old: 'they seemed completely out of place'; new: 'she was completely out of place.' Singular vs plural.

TextENGNew: ``...Which is exactly why she stood out so much.``
changeReason: Old: 'Which is why they stood out to me'; new: 'Which is exactly why she stood out so much.' Singular vs plural.

TextENGNew: ``...Perhaps you should tell us more.``
For the first time, Kasai-san leaned slightly forward, assuming a listening posture. Seeing that, Shion's expression also turned somewhat more serious as she faced us.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, altering the specificity of the passage.

TextENGNew: ``...I see.@k@vS14/15/2115000018. That is certainly a troubling report.``
Kasai-san had listened in silence throughout Rika-chan's careful explanation. When she finished, he pressed his lips into a thin line and nodded several times before speaking.
``...So, what do you want us to do about it?``

``We need you to make it so Rina can't get her hands on the Sonozaki tribute money.@k@vS14/01/210100677. Rina works at a Sonozaki-affiliated club, so with the Sonozakis' power, shutting down her operation should be simple enough, right?``
changeReason: Old: 'We need to prevent this Rina woman from going after the treasury funds'; new: 'We need you to make it so Rina can't get her hands on the Sonozaki tribute money.' 'Treasury funds' vs 'tribute money' (JPN 上納金 = tribute).

TextENGNew: ``Well, I suppose...``

``And even if that's not feasible...@k@vS14/01/210100679. we figured it was something worth bringing to a Sonozaki -- to you, Shion.@k@vS14/01/210100680. It's hard to say this doesn't concern your family.``
changeReason: New translation adds reference to shion, altering the specificity of the passage.

TextENGNew: ``...If that's your reasoning, then why not tell my sister?@k@vS14/06/210600290. Don't you think you're going about this out of order?``
``Well...@k@vS14/01/210100682. I figured you'd know more about this kind of thing, Shion.``
``Huh?@k@vS14/06/210600292. Know more? Why would I?``

``Shion, you said you've worked part-time at various Sonozaki-affiliated places, right?@k@vS14/01/210100684. So I figured you'd be better informed about the inner workings of those establishments, tribute payments, and all that.``
I answered reflexively, but I was confident the logic held up.
Mion was technically the acting head of the Sonozaki family, but from what I'd heard, Shion was more familiar with affairs in Okinomiya, given the distance.
What's more, unconfirmed intelligence about troubling activity on the ground likely wouldn't even reach Mion.
``And for all we know, you might have even worked at that particular club yourself, Shion.``
``That club... on Flower Road?@k@vS14/06/210600294. Me?``
``...There is absolutely no way I'd work there!``
I nodded along vigorously. I'd meant it as a joke... but wait, why was Rika-chan nodding too?
``...You've never worked there?``

``No!@k@vS14/06/210600297. ...Kei-chan, Rika-chama, are you trying to pick a fight?@k@vS14/06/210600298. If a minor like me worked at a place like that, I'd get a straight red card!@k@vS14/06/210600299. Honestly...``
``...Though she did once ask me to let her work there.@k@vS14/15/2115000020. Said the hourly wage was good.``
``Shut up!``
...So she had considered it after all. I knew it.
Shion cleared her throat and composed herself, then fixed me with a searching look as she spoke.
``My part-time work aside.@k@vS14/06/210600303. ...What's your real objective here, Kei-chan? What do you stand to gain?``
``If we stop Rina from taking the tribute money, there must be some benefit for you two, right?@k@vS14/06/210600305. So what is it?@k@vS14/06/210600306. I'd very much like to hear it.``
``First of all, how do you two even know about something like this?@k@vS14/06/210600308. What's your connection to this Rina woman?@k@vS14/06/210600309. And what purpose does stopping her serve?``
``...Given the gravity of the situation, I'm sorry, but I can't just take your word for it unless you make these things clear.``
Put on the spot like that, I found myself at a loss for words.
Shion was a sharp one, so naturally she wasn't going to believe us easily. Though I could hardly blame her -- I was only half-convinced myself...
I decided to invoke the name of the person behind Rina.
changeReason: Old: 'I decided to bring up the background of the person named Rina'; new: 'I decided to invoke the name of the person behind Rina.' JPN リナの背後にいる人物 = 'the person behind/backing Rina', not Rina's own background.

TextENGNew: ``Rina Mamiya is living with Teppei Houjou.@k@vS14/01/210100690. I simply don't want anything that benefits those two.``
``Don't want to benefit them... huh.@k@vS14/06/210600312. You know, Kei-chan, there's a much simpler solution for that?``
Shion's eyes flashed with a sharp, predatory glint. ...Behind the smile she tried to maintain, an undisguisable hatred was surfacing.

``Forget something as roundabout as what you're suggesting.@k@vS14/06/210600314. We just ambush them, catch them, and make them understand.@k@vS14/06/210600315. What happens to anyone who crosses the Sonozakis... heh heh heh!``
changeReason: Old: 'Not one as soft as what you're proposing'; new: 'Forget something as roundabout as what you're suggesting.' JPN 甘っちょろい = 'soft/naive', both capture it but differently.

TextENGNew: ``...Just kidding!``
``Well, my point is, going to the police isn't the only form of punishment.@k@vS14/06/210600318. Wouldn't you say, Kasai?``
changeReason: Old: 'just reporting this to the police wouldn't be enough to punish them'; new: 'going to the police isn't the only form of punishment.' Different logical framing - old says police is insufficient; new says police is one of many options. JPN 警察沙汰にするだけが断罪にはならない = 'going to the police isn't the only punishment'.

TextENGNew: ``...Shion-san.``
Kasai-san placed a hand on his sunglasses and turned to Shion with a reproving look. Realizing she might have taken the joke too far, Shion shrugged and drained her glass of water to calm herself.
``In any case, regarding Rina...@k@vS14/15/2115000023. As a matter of fact, I had already been keeping tabs on her for related reasons.``
``...Oh? Really?``
Seeing the slight look of surprise on Shion's face, Kasai-san quietly nodded.
``Rina has apparently been having disputes with her colleagues over money lending, and she's been showing up to work less and less.``
``And it's not just her colleagues -- she seems to have racked up considerable debts all over town. There are very few people who react kindly to hearing her name.``
``Hmm...@k@vS14/06/210600321. But that alone doesn't...``

``True enough...@k@vS14/15/2115000027. However, she's been cozying up to some of the younger members of the organization in a suspicious manner. It struck me as odd, given she supposedly has no money.``
``Living large despite being in debt.@k@vS14/06/210600323. ...Is what you're saying.``
``And one more thing.@k@vS14/15/2115000029. ...You mentioned she was spotted at the library. I'd actually heard the same report.``
``I thought it was peculiar at the time, but...@k@vS14/15/2115000031. hearing this now... it all makes sense.``
Kasai-san nodded, as if the pieces had clicked into place. Seeing that, Shion spoke up.
I shifted my approach and addressed Kasai-san directly.
``Have you noticed anything unusual about Rina's behavior recently?``
``Unusual... how?``
``For instance... maybe she's been spending money freely despite barely showing up to work?@k@vS14/01/210100693. Or maybe she's started associating with people you don't recognize...``
Kasai-san tilted his head and thought in silence for a while, as if organizing his memories. Then he slowly turned to Shion and, after confirming her nod, opened his mouth.
``If that's the case, then...``
``Hmm, Kasai... does something come to mind?``
...Just as I thought. His answer gave me confidence, and I leaned in further.
``Rina has apparently been having disputes with her colleagues over money lending, and she's been showing up to work less and less.``
``And it's not just her colleagues -- she seems to have racked up considerable debts all over town. Very few people react kindly to hearing her name.``
``Hmm...@k@vS14/06/210600326. But that alone isn't enough to...``

``True enough...@k@vS14/15/2115000039. However, she's been associating with some of the younger members of the organization in a suspicious manner. Given that she supposedly has no money, I've been thinking it was strange.``
``Spending freely despite being in debt.@k@vS14/06/210600328. ...Is what you're saying.``

``And one more thing.@k@vS14/15/2115000041. ...You mentioned she was spotted at the library. I'd actually heard the same report myself.@k@vS14/15/2115000042. I thought it was peculiar at the time, but...@k@vS14/15/2115000043. hearing this now... it all makes sense.``
Kasai-san nodded, as if the pieces had clicked into place. Seeing that, Shion spoke up.
``Wait, Kasai?@k@vS14/06/210600330. You're actually taking what Rika-chama and Kei-chan are saying seriously?``
``There could be something to it.@k@vS14/15/2115000045. And if someone were to get their hands on our tribute funds so easily, it would be a matter of honor for the organization.``
``...Ah, excuse me, Kasai-san.``
I jumped in at the word 'honor.'
changeReason: Significant difference in content length suggests added or omitted information in the new translation.

TextENGNew: ``If at all possible, I'd like you to not do anything to Rina.``
``Not do anything...?@k@vS14/15/2115000047. What do you mean by that?``
Kasai-san's eyes suddenly glinted behind his sunglasses. I held my breath under the weight of his presence, but this was something I absolutely had to say.
``...Please don't kill her.@k@vS14/01/210100696. That's what I mean.``
``Huh?@k@vS14/06/210600333. ...U-um, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/06/210600334. I've completely lost the thread of what you're saying, you know??``
Shion's face scrunched up in open bewilderment as she shot me an irritated glare.

And who could blame her? I wasn't just meddling in an organization's internal affairs -- I was pleading for the life of a complete stranger who wasn't even worth saving. It was nothing short of bizarre.
But before I could say anything else, Kasai-san quietly adjusted his sunglasses and spoke with cold finality.

``...I'm afraid that is something I cannot agree to.@k@vS14/15/2115000049. We can't allow petty criminals like that to make fools of us. It would be a disgrace.@k@vS14/15/2115000050. Facing the consequences is simply the way things work in our world.``
Roughly the answer I'd expected. Strict about the things that demanded strictness, I suppose.
...Still, the fact that I was able to speak directly with the people involved made this easier than I'd anticipated. It meant I could convey my intentions precisely.
``I understand that.@k@vS14/01/210100698. ...But please.@k@vS14/01/210100699. I'm not asking you to forgive them. I want this whole situation to be rendered moot -- as if it never happened.``
``As if it never... happened?@k@vS14/15/2115000052. That's simply not possible. In the first place--``
``In other words, if Rina and her people abandon the heist before it even starts, the money is never taken and your honor is never compromised.@k@vS14/01/210100701. Right?``
``Well... I suppose...``
``...So? Forgive me for interrupting, but...@k@vS14/06/210600336. this brings us to the obvious question.@k@vS14/06/210600337. Why on earth are you trying to help a scoundrel like Rina, Kei-chan?``
``...It's because of Satoko.``
I drew a deep breath and looked Shion straight in the eyes. I could feel Rika-chan gazing up at me anxiously from beside me, but I decided this was the moment to lay it all out.
Shion was one of us. That was precisely why, when the problem ran this deep, she'd brought Kasai-san along to ensure the information was relayed accurately.
Meeting trust with sincerity was, I believed, a natural obligation.
``This Rina woman is connected to Satoko's uncle -- the one who treated her so terribly...@k@vS14/01/210100704. She's deeply involved with Teppei.``
``...What do you mean by that?``
I faltered for a moment, unsure how to explain. I didn't know much about the man myself, so I worried my explanation might come off wrong.
``...Rika-chan came to me about this.@k@vS14/01/210100707. She asked if there might be a way to resolve things peacefully.``
``...That's right.@k@vS14/05/210500528. I thought it would be dangerous to let this escalate.``
Rika-chan chimed in to supplement my explanation. ...But Shion kept tilting her head skeptically, fixing us with a probing stare as she tried to divine our true intentions.
``Frankly, I can't fathom why you'd go this far to shield a complete stranger.@k@vS14/06/210600341. Reward the good, punish the wicked -- that's the fundamental law of honor.``
...If it had come to this, there was no choice.
Bracing myself for even more suspicion, I laid bare the truth.
``This Rina woman is connected to Satoko's uncle -- the one who treated her so horribly...@k@vS14/01/210100709. She's deeply involved with Teppei.``
``Excuse me?@k@vS14/06/210600343. ...What exactly do you mean by that?``
I faltered for a moment, unsure how to explain. I didn't know much about the man myself, so I worried my explanation might come off wrong.
``Rina and this Teppei are partners.``
Kasai-san stepped in to clarify for the bewildered Shion.
``I think you're already aware that Teppei Houjou is a notorious thug.@k@vS14/15/2115000056. He lives right here in Okinomiya.``
``...It appears Rina and Teppei have been running a marriage fraud scheme as their livelihood.``

In other words, the woman would approach a mark and reel him in romantically. Then, once the relationship was established, the man would barge in demanding, 'Stay away from my woman,' and shake the victim down for money.
``Lately, these fraud operations with Rina seem to be Teppei Houjou's sole source of income.``
``So basically... Teppei Houjou's livelihood depends entirely on Rina...``
Shion nodded, looking as though the picture had finally come together.
``What if... Rina were to take the money and skip town?``

``If Rina were to steal the tribute money and flee Okinomiya...@k@vS14/01/210100713. no, even if she were caught and dealt with, Teppei would lose his only source of income and be out on the street immediately.@k@vS14/01/210100714. He wouldn't be able to make rent before long, and he'd have trouble just scraping by.``
``That's true, but...``

``In that case... Teppei Houjou would have no choice but to go back to his house in Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/01/210100716. ...And if that happened, he'd drag Satoko back to live with him.@k@vS14/01/210100717. And the result of that... I'm sure you can imagine.``
The moment she heard that, Shion's complexion drained of color.
Rika-chan and Shion knew far better than I did what Satoko had endured at the hands of her uncle and his wife.
Satoko had finally gotten her vitality back living together with Rika-chan. If she were to end up with her uncle again, back in the same nightmare as last year... it was all too easy to imagine.
``...Ah, ahaha, Kei-chan! You're overthinking things!@k@vS14/06/210600351. What is this, some kind of TV drama?``
After a brief silence... Shion forced out a dry laugh, trying to play it off. But lurking in those eyes of hers was a dangerous, murderous intent.
He's still alive, then. One half of the pair who tormented and cornered Satoko.
She'd let him off the hook while he stayed in Okinomiya, but she'd never actually forgiven him... and all this time, he'd been living it up without a care in the world.
Unforgivable. Then she'd eliminate him right along with this Rina woman...!
I could see it clearly -- those pitch-black emotions churning inside Shion's heart.
``...What is it, Kei-chan?``
``It's fine, I'm listening.``
Shion said that, but her eyes were staring off in a completely different direction. ...As if to say she'd already heard everything she needed to hear.
...After that, we made some idle small talk before leaving the shop.
Everyone had been wearing a pleasant expression, but... the look on Shion's face as she departed weighed heavily on my mind.
